{{Quote|I understand that Slam is a bit of a dandy.|[[Tyro Caladian]]|<ref name="JQ:CotG" />}}
'''Roper Slam''', a native of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core world]] of [[Mamendin]], was a young [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] con man, and founder of the [[Slams|Slam gang]], a notorious gang of expert thieves made particularly infamous by its heist of the [[Vuma/Legends|Vuma]] treasury. He owned a [[Ubrikkian star yacht]].

In [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]], in an attempt to apprehend [[Jenna Zan Arbor]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] took on the identity of Slam while serving [[Mission to Romin|undercover]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Romin]].<ref name="JQ:CotG" /> By awkward chance, Kenobi—while at an extravagant reception for the world's wealthy, hosted by its corrupt tyrant-ruler, "Great Leader [[Roy Teda|Teda]]"—met Slam, sharing with him over idle chat a glass of the favorite Romin beverage, [[quint-berry juice]]. Kenobi described Slam as having "a tired, handsome face."<ref name="JQ:CotG" />

Having escaped yet again from prison—the [[Greylands Security Complex]] on [[Tentator]]—the Slams had fled for refuge to Romin, where Teda harbored galactic criminals in exchange for hefty "contributions" to the tyrant's "charities." Because the Slams had been imprisoned at the time the mission to Romin began, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] had actually confiscated Roper Slam's star yacht from the [[Confiscation Station|Greylands Confiscation Station]]. But due to a revolt of the people under the leadership of [[Joylin]] and his [[Citizens' Resistance]], the Slams were reunited with their beloved ship. Joylin had allowed Teda to escape with Zan Arbor, who sent out a petition that ''both'' Slam gangs answered—both the impostors ''and'' the real criminals—to give her and Teda transport offworld and join them in a highly profitable heist on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] that they had masterminded with [[Granta Omega]].<ref name="JQ:CotG" />

When the Jedi were caught impersonating the criminals, Zan Arbor ordered Teda's army officers to haul them off to prison while she made her preparations with the ''real'' Slams. Joylin had already fueled and made ready for departure the Slams' yacht on the morning after the people's revolt, and it was the only transport the Resistance was permitting to leave the planet. As such, the Slams were Zan Arbor and Teda's only choice of escape. The Slams were successful in navigating their ship out of Romin airspace and stealing for the planet of [[Falleen (planet)/Legends|Falleen]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], there to continue their plans with Omega [[Attack on the Senate Building|against]] the galactic capital and its [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] [[Senate Building/Legends|Building]].<ref name="JQ:CotG" /><ref name="JQ:TFP">''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]''</ref> In that attempted [[Bank of the Core Plaza]] heist and [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] murder operation (in which [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and hundreds of Senators were targeted), Roper Slam and his female cohort [[Valadon]] were captured again and returned to prison.<ref name="JQ:TFP" />
